description.en: AnArrayBuffer, aTypedArray, or aDataView. TheHKDF specificationstates that adding salt "adds significantly to the strength of HKDF". Ideally, the salt is a random or pseudo-random value with the same length as the output of the digest function. Unlike the input key material passed intoderiveKey(), salt does not need to be kept secret.

description.en: AnArrayBuffer, aTypedArray, or aDataView. This should be a random or pseudo-random value of at least 16 bytes. Unlike the input key material passed intoderiveKey(),saltdoes not need to be kept secret.

description.en: Alonginteger representing the length of the random salt to use, in bytes.RFC 3447says that "Typical salt lengths" are either 0 or the length of the output of thedigest algorithmthat was selected when this key wasgenerated. For example, if you useSHA-256as the digest algorithm, this could be 32.The maximum size ofsaltLengthis given by:jsMath.ceil((keySizeInBits - 1) / 8) - digestSizeInBytes - 2;So for a key length of 2048 bits and a digest output size of 32 bytes, the maximum size would be 222.

description.en: Returns aSubtleCryptoobject providing access to common cryptographic primitives, like hashing, signing, encryption, or decryption.
